etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "dirt") + phonetic 丰(豐) (fēng, "seedling") + semantic 寸(寸) (cùn, "hand") -> marking boundaries with seedlings -> seal; close
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 丰(豐) (fēng, "seedling") as a phonetic element suggesting the sound, and 土(土) (tǔ, "dirt") and 寸(寸) (cùn, "hand") as semantic elements. The combination symbolizes marking a boundary by planting seedlings in the ground, which is a way of sealing or enclosing an area. This concept naturally extends to sealing or closing something.

examples.chinese: 他亲手封好信封(他親手封好信封)。
我们需要把窗户封住(我們需要把窗戶封住)，以防寒风进入(以防寒風進入)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào bǎ chuānghù fēng zhù, yǐ fáng hánfēng jìnrù.
examples.english: We need to seal the windows to prevent the cold wind from entering.

examples.chinese: 信件已经被谨慎地密封(信件已經被謹慎地密封)。
examples.pinyin: Xìnjiàn yǐjīng bèi jǐnshèn de mìfēng.
examples.english: The letter has been carefully sealed.
